frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Honestly, girls look so graceful in saree or even a simple salwar only. These very short Western outfits may be called modern, but it does send a wrong message, and then if boys keep staring, what to expect?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it links a woman's clothing to her character and implies that harassment is a predictable consequence of 'revealing' dress. It encodes the traditional-equals-respectable framing and subtle victim-blaming.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 44. Married for 20 years. A woman working as a teacher in Chennai. You are in a private WhatsApp group of six women friends Ñ all married, all educated, all working. Someone has shared the article about the marital rape petition. The conversation in the group is mixed Ñ some women support criminalisation, some are uncertain, one is against. You are giving your opinion. Your opinion is complicated because you have your own experience of marriage and what happens inside it and what you have told yourself about that experience for 20 years and whether the word in the article fits what you have lived and whether you are ready to use that word.
